---
title: 'Watching the wind...'
date: '2020-02-01T16:01:00.0010000+00:00'
draft: false
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '335167150601362692'
blogger_filename: '/2020/02/watching-wind.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Musings'
---

I'm watching the wind take tall strong pine trees and toss them back and forth, whip my American Flag to and fro violently...<div><br></div><div>Yet...</div><div><br></div><div>Am I?</div><div><br></div><div>Am I watching the wind?</div><div><br></div><div>I am watching its affects. The wind is invisible.</div><div><br></div><div>As I read Philip Yancey this morning, I find I am in need of a theology shift. I can carry forward some of the beautiful lessons of yester-year's moves of God without carrying forward their excesses.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>I can look to Faith and Healing as true, real, and necessary parts of living in God.</div><div><br></div><div>But not to the exclusion of Pain and Suffering.</div><div><br></div><div>Both the Heights of Pleasure/Reward and the Depths of Pain/Suffering are parts of the life we live on this broken Earth.</div><div><br></div><div>In a broken world, things break.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>That's it. That's the cause of suffering. It isn't God's will that you suffer but the world is broken and His promise of ultimate restoration is future-tense.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>When Jesus steps foot on the Earth, he will make things new. But even then, 1,000 years later there will be a second round of suffering before the world is re-made in His image.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>So until the ULTIMATE restoration of all things into His will... There will be Pain. It will be a part of life. God will walk through the valley of the shadow of death with us.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Note: Walk through, not avoid.</div><div><br></div><div>But out of that experience, he also brings comfort, banquet table, and anointing.</div><div><br></div><div>Healing and Death.</div><div><br></div><div>Pleasure and Pain.</div><div><br></div><div>These are all part of the experience.</div><div><br></div><div>I can find Him in all of it.</div><div><br></div><div>If I turn to Him in both, both will be more fulfilling and rewarding, despite the Pain.</div><div><br></div><div>I may not always see Him in every moment, sometimes His wind is very still.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Sometimes He will rush in and move things, other times He will be a gentle touch, other times He will be so still I am not aware, but He is there.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Always there.&nbsp;&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>If I can breathe then I know the air of His presence is there, felt or not.<br></div><div><br></div><div>Selah.</div><div><br></div><div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;">
  <a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1580572853159296-0-7a505c842fe9.png" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;">
    <img border="0" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1580572853159296-0-7a505c842fe9.png" width="400">
  </a>
</div><br></div>
